creator: david@catwhisker.org
text:
Created attachment 147131
Build log showing last failure

Running: FreeBSD g1-235.catwhisker.org 9.3-STABLE FreeBSD 9.3-STABLE #93  r271233M/271306:903502: Tue Sep  9 04:57:08 PDT 2014     root@g1-235.catwhisker.org:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/CANARY  i386

Initial failure was during "portmaster -ad" run (which eventually did perform:
        Upgrade of GeoIP-1.6.2 to GeoIP-1.6.2_1
        Upgrade of ca_root_nss-3.16.3 to ca_root_nss-3.17
        Upgrade of libao-1.2.0 to libao-1.2.0_1
        Upgrade of nspr-4.10.6 to nspr-4.10.7
        Upgrade of ffmpeg-2.2.4_5,1 to ffmpeg-2.2.4_6,1
        Upgrade of glib-1.2.10_13 to glib12-1.2.10_14
        Upgrade of gstreamer-ffmpeg-0.10.13_2 to gstreamer-ffmpeg-0.10.13_3
        Upgrade of libv4l-0.8.8_1 to libv4l-0.8.8_2
        Upgrade of libxul-24.7.0 to libxul-24.8.0
        Upgrade of p5-IO-Socket-SSL-1.997_2 to p5-IO-Socket-SSL-1.998
        Upgrade of p5-Net-DNS-0.78 to p5-Net-DNS-0.79
        Upgrade of tex-dvipdfmx-20140317 to tex-dvipdfmx-20140317_1
        Upgrade of xf86-input-keyboard-1.8.0_2 to xf86-input-keyboard-1.8.0_3
        Upgrade of magicpoint-1.13a_10 to magicpoint-1.13a_11
        Upgrade of xorg-nestserver-1.12.4_8,1 to xorg-nestserver-1.14.7,1
)

In looking through the build log ("portmaster" invocation was within script(1), as usual), I noted that the compiler used was g++47, which struck me as odd: I thought I had recalled that recent firefox was built with clang++ by defaut (when it's available -- and clang has been my "system compiler" since just after BSDCan 2012).  A look at www/firefox/Makefile showed:

# avoid clang 3.2/3.3 crash in js/src/jsgc.cpp on refillFreeList<(js::AllowGC)1>
# http://svnweb.freebsd.org/changeset/base/255804
.if ${CHOSEN_COMPILER_TYPE} == clang && ${ARCH} == i386 && ${OSVERSION} < 100005
6
. if ${PORT_OPTIONS:MOPTIMIZED_CFLAGS}
BROKEN=         Cannot build with OPTIMIZED_CFLAGS option due to \
                a ${CHOSEN_COMPILER_TYPE} bug: unset the option or \
                use FAVORITE_COMPILER=gcc in Makefile.local/make.conf
# apply workaround only for -O2 or greater
. elif ${CXXFLAGS:M-O*} && ! ${CXXFLAGS:M-O[01]}
.  if ${MACHINE_CPU:Msse2}
CXXFLAGS+=      -msse2
.  else
CXXFLAGS+=      -O1
.  endif
. endif
.endif


Given that hint, I ran "make showconfig" in www/firefox, saw that I did have OPTIMIZED_CFLAGS set, so I ran "make config" an un-set it, then re-tried.  The failure persisted (and g++47 was still used).

So I went to www/firefox and (maunually) issued "make clean && make MAKE_JOBS_UNSAFE=yes"; I have attached the part of the typescript file that shows this last attempt.

For reference:
g1-235(9.3-S)[3] cd /usr/ports/www/firefox
g1-235(9.3-S)[4] make showconfig
===> The following configuration options are available for firefox-32.0,1:
     DBUS=off: D-Bus IPC system support
     DEBUG=off: Build with debugging support
     DTRACE=off: Build with DTrace probes
     GCONF=off: GConf configuration backend support
     GIO=off: GIO for file I/O
     GNOMEUI=off: libgnomeui support module
     GNOMEVFS2=off: GnomeVFS2 (virtual file system) support
     GSTREAMER=off: Multimedia support via GStreamer
     LIBPROXY=off: Proxy support via libproxy
     LOGGING=off: Additional log messages
     OPTIMIZED_CFLAGS=off: Use extra compiler optimizations
     PGO=off: Use Profile-Guided Optimization
     PROFILE=off: Build with profiling support
     TEST=off: Build and/or run tests
====> Options available for the multi AUDIO: you have to choose at least one of them
     ALSA=on: ALSA audio architecture support
     PULSEAUDIO=off: PulseAudio sound server support
===> Use 'make config' to modify these settings
g1-235(9.3-S)[5] make -V CFLAGS
-O2 -pipe -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib/gcc47 -fno-strict-aliasing
